titleStmt.title: Letter from the President and Fellows of the Royal College of Physicians to Charles Benson, 11 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: The President and Fellows of the Royal College of Physicians

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from the Royal College of Physicians of Ireland (RCPI) to Charles Molyneux Benson (1877-1919) informing Benson that the Joint Committee felt it could not support the new nursing college scheme without proper Irish representation. This resolution was forwarded to Sir Arthur Stanley without the usual approval of the College due to the urgency of the matter.

msIdentifier.repository: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

correspAction.persName: The President and Fellows of the Royal College of Physicians
correspAction.placeName: Royal College of Physicians, Kildare Street, Dublin, Ireland
Royal College of Surgeons of Ireland, St Stephen's Green, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
11th APRIL 16. C.M. Benson,Esq., M.D., Secretary to Council, R.C.S.I. Conference re College of Nursing : Preliminary report. Dear Sir, The Conference Committee of the two Royal Colleges
re the College of Nursing has held two meetings. At the
last of these meetings, which was held on the 4th inst., the
Committee met a number of ladies connected with the Nursing
profession in Ireland. At that meeting the ladies present
expressed their views as to the proposed College and what
it effect would be on the nursing profession in Ireland. The Committee was informed that the College had been
established and that steps were to be taken on April 7th
to formulate the conditions of admission to it. In view of the urgency of the matter and of the fact that
there was no representation of Irish nursing on the
governing body of the College the Committee adopted the
following resolution and directed me to forward it ai once
to the Hon.Arthur Stanley.
Resolved : 'that this Committee representing the Royal Colleges of
Physicians and Surgeons of Ireland inform Mr. Stanley that it cannot
recommend Irish Nurses to support the College of Nursing Ltd., unless
at least one fifth of the supreme governing body of that College
consists of Irish representatives.' No answer has yet been received from Mr. Stanley. The Committee felt bound, owing to the urgency of the matter, to
take the unusual step of making this representation to Mr. Stanley
without consulting the Colleges. I may add that at the last meeting
of the Royal College of Physicians the President and Fellows approved
the action taken by the Committee. Yours faithfully,
